text.title: Performance Domains Overview Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the Performance Domains Overview methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Report MUST score ALL eight performance domains; partial reports are rejected.
rule.rationale: domains are interdependent; partial reports invite cherry-picking and hide systemic issues.

rule.statement: Each domain score MUST cite >=2 verbatim evidence anchors (status report line, retro action, risk entry, transcript timestamp).
rule.rationale: scores without evidence drift to assessor's mood and lose credibility.

rule.statement: Score scale MUST be the canonical 1-5 (1=systemic gap, 3=adequate, 5=mature); free-form scores are rejected.
rule.rationale: free-form scores cannot be compared across projects or quarters.

rule.statement: Every score <=2 MUST carry at least one named remediation action (owner + due date); low scores without remediations are rejected.
rule.rationale: low scores without action become wallpaper and the assessment loses purpose.

rule.statement: Average score across 8 domains > 4.5 requires explicit sponsor sign-off; assessors anchoring everything to 4-5 is a known calibration anti-pattern.
rule.rationale: flatter assessments fail to identify gaps; calibration drift makes them useless.

rule.statement: Score per domain MUST consider both presence (does the practice exist?) and effectiveness (does it work?); binary 'practice exists -> 5' is rejected.
